=== Internal Structure === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).
== References == * Nishihakayama Kofun: Furuichi Kofun Group Research Report III, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1997.

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nishihakayama Kofun |image= |location=Osaka Prefecture, Fujidera City, Aoyama 1-chome |位置= |type=Square-shaped kofun |area=A11roximately 20m per side |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設=Iron artifact burial facility |被葬者= |burial goods=Numerous iron burial goods |史跡指定= |特記事項=Buried kofun }} '''Nishihakayama Kofun''' (西墓山古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Aoyama, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The mound is presumed to have been square-shaped and is one of the kofun that typeed part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a [[World Heritage Site|UNESCO World other_designation site]].
The existence of the kofun was confirmed during an excavation conducted due to redevelopment for residential housing. A row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and an iron artifact burial structure were discovered, revealing a wealth of iron burial goods.
== Overview == The kofun was located west of the {{ill|Makuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}'s front section and just south of its associated {{ill|Jōgenjiyama Kofun|ja|浄元寺山古墳}}, a satellite mound. In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal Structure === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nishihakayama Kofun |image= |location=Osaka Prefecture, Fujidera City, Aoyama 1-chome |位置= |type=Square-shaped kofun |area=A11roximately 20m per side |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設=Iron artifact burial facility |被葬者= |burial goods=Numerous iron burial goods |史跡指定= |特記事項=Buried kofun }} '''Nishihakayama Kofun''' (西墓山古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Aoyama, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The mound is presumed to have been square-shaped and is one of the kofun that typeed part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a [[World Heritage Site|UNESCO World other_designation site]].
The existence of the kofun was confirmed during an excavation conducted due to redevelopment for residential housing. A row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and an iron artifact burial structure were discovered, revealing a wealth of iron burial goods.
== Overview == The kofun was located west of the {{ill|Makuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}'s front section and just south of its associated {{ill|Jōgenjiyama Kofun|ja|浄元寺山古墳}}, a satellite mound. In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).
== References == * Nishihakayama Kofun: Furuichi Kofun Group Research Report III, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1997.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,0,98,146.html About Nishihakayama Kofun] (Fujidera City)

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nishihakayama Kofun |image= |location=Osaka Prefecture, Fujidera City, Aoyama 1-chome |位置= |type=Square-shaped kofun |area=A11roximately 20m per side |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設=Iron artifact burial facility |被葬者= |burial goods=Numerous iron burial goods |史跡指定= |特記事項=Buried kofun }} '''Nishihakayama Kofun''' (西墓山古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Aoyama, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The mound is presumed to have been square-shaped and is one of the kofun that typeed part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a [[World Heritage Site|UNESCO World other_designation site]].
The existence of the kofun was confirmed during an excavation conducted due to redevelopment for residential housing. A row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and an iron artifact burial structure were discovered, revealing a wealth of iron burial goods.
== Overview == The kofun was located west of the {{ill|Makuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}'s front section and just south of its associated {{ill|Jōgenjiyama Kofun|ja|浄元寺山古墳}}, a satellite mound. In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).
== References == * Nishihakayama Kofun: Furuichi Kofun Group Research Report III, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1997.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,0,98,146.html About Nishihakayama Kofun] (Fujidera City)

The mound is presumed to have been square-shaped and is one of the kofun that typeed part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a [[World Heritage Site|UNESCO World other_designation site]].
The existence of the kofun was confirmed during an excavation conducted due to redevelopment for residential housing. A row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and an iron artifact burial structure were discovered, revealing a wealth of iron burial goods.
== Overview == The kofun was located west of the {{ill|Makuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}'s front section and just south of its associated {{ill|Jōgenjiyama Kofun|ja|浄元寺山古墳}}, a satellite mound. In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).
== References == * Nishihakayama Kofun: Furuichi Kofun Group Research Report III, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1997.

Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nishihakayama Kofun |image= |location=Osaka Prefecture, Fujidera City, Aoyama 1-chome |位置= |type=Square-shaped kofun |area=A11roximately 20m per side |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設=Iron artifact burial facility |被葬者= |burial goods=Numerous iron burial goods |史跡指定= |特記事項=Buried kofun }} '''Nishihakayama Kofun''' (西墓山古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Aoyama, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The mound is presumed to have been square-shaped and is one of the kofun that typeed part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a [[World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Historic Site site]].
The existence of the kofun was confirmed during an excavation conducted due to redevelopment for residential housing. A row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and an iron artifact burial structure were discovered, revealing a wealth of iron burial goods.
== Overview == The kofun was located west of the {{ill|Makuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}'s front section and just south of its associated {{ill|Jōgenjiyama Kofun|ja|浄元寺山古墳}}, a satellite mound. In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nishihakayama Kofun |image= |location=Osaka Prefecture, Fujidera City, Aoyama 1-chome |位置= |type=Square-shaped kofun |area=A11roximately 20m per side |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設=Iron artifact burial facility |被葬者= |burial goods=Numerous iron burial goods |史跡指定= |特記事項=Buried kofun }} '''Nishihakayama Kofun''' (西墓山古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Aoyama, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The mound is presumed to have been square-shaped and is one of the kofun that typeed part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a [[World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Historic Site site]].
The existence of the kofun was confirmed during an excavation conducted due to redevelopment for residential housing. A row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} and an iron artifact burial structure were discovered, revealing a wealth of iron burial goods.
== Overview == The kofun was located west of the {{ill|Makuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun}}'s front section and just south of its associated {{ill|Jōgenjiyama Kofun|ja|浄元寺山古墳}}, a satellite mound. In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).
== References == * Nishihakayama Kofun: Furuichi Kofun Group Research Report III, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1997.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,0,98,146.html About Nishihakayama Kofun] (Fujidera City)
<!--Nishihayama Kofun-->
<!--Nishihayama Kofun--> == See Also == * [[Aisell Shura Hall]] * [[Igenoyama Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nishihakayama Kofun |image= |location=Osaka Prefecture, Fujidera City, Aoyama 1-chome |位置= |type=Square-shaped kofun |area=A11roximately 20m per side |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設=Iron artifact burial facility |被葬者= |burial goods=Numerous iron burial goods |史跡指定= |特記事項=Buried kofun }} '''Nishihakayama Kofun''' (西墓山古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Aoyama, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The mound is presumed to have been square-shaped and is one of the kofun that typeed part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, a [[World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Historic Site site]].
The existence of the kofun was confirmed during an excavation conducted due to redevelopment for residential housing. A row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} and an iron artifact burial structure were discovered, revealing a wealth of iron burial goods.
== Overview == The kofun was located west of the {{ill|Makuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft}}'s front section and just south of its associated {{ill|Jōgenjiyama Kofun|ja|浄元寺山古墳|12=draft}}, a satellite mound. In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣|12=draft}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=draft}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).
== References == * Nishihakayama Kofun: Furuichi Kofun Group Research Report III, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1997.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,0,98,146.html About Nishihakayama Kofun] (Fujidera City)

=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣|12=draft}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=draft}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).

Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.

The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.

Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nishihakayama Kofun |image= |location=Osaka Prefecture, Fujidera City, Aoyama 1-chome |位置= |type=Square-shaped kofun |area=A11roximately 20m per side |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設=Iron artifact burial facility |被葬者= |burial goods=Numerous iron burial goods |史跡指定= |特記事項=Buried kofun }} '''Nishihakayama Kofun''' (西墓山古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Aoyama, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The mound is presumed to have been square-shaped and is one of the kofun that typeed part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, a [[World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Historic Site site]].
The existence of the kofun was confirmed during an excavation conducted due to redevelopment for residential housing. A row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} and an iron artifact burial structure were discovered, revealing a wealth of iron burial goods.
== Overview == The kofun was located west of the {{ill|Makuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft}}'s front section and just south of its associated {{ill|Jōgenjiyama Kofun|ja|浄元寺山古墳|12=draft}}, a satellite mound. In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣|12=draft}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=draft}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).
== References == * Nishihakayama Kofun: Furuichi Kofun Group Research Report III, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1997.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,0,98,146.html About Nishihakayama Kofun] (Fujidera City)

In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣|12=draft}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=draft}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).
== References == * Nishihakayama Kofun: Furuichi Kofun Group Research Report III, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1997.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,0,98,146.html About Nishihakayama Kofun] (Fujidera City)

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nishihakayama Kofun |image= |location=Osaka Prefecture, Fujidera City, Aoyama 1-chome |位置= |type=Square-shaped kofun |area=A11roximately 20m per side |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設=Iron artifact burial facility |被葬者= |burial goods=Numerous iron burial goods |史跡指定= |特記事項=Buried kofun }} '''Nishihakayama Kofun''' (西墓山古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Aoyama, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The mound is presumed to have been square-shaped and is one of the kofun that typeed part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, a [[World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Historic Site site]].
The existence of the kofun was confirmed during an excavation conducted due to redevelopment for residential housing. A row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} and an iron artifact burial structure were discovered, revealing a wealth of iron burial goods.
== Overview == The kofun was located west of the {{ill|Makuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft}}'s front section and just south of its associated {{ill|Jōgenjiyama Kofun|ja|浄元寺山古墳|12=draft}}, a satellite mound. In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣|12=draft}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=draft}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nishihakayama Kofun |image= |location=Osaka Prefecture, Fujidera City, Aoyama 1-chome |位置= |type=Square-shaped kofun |area=A11roximately 20m per side |築造年代=5th century |埋葬施設=Iron artifact burial facility |被葬者= |burial goods=Numerous iron burial goods |史跡指定= |特記事項=Buried kofun }} '''Nishihakayama Kofun''' (西墓山古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Aoyama, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The mound is presumed to have been square-shaped and is one of the kofun that typeed part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, a [[World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Historic Site site]].
The existence of the kofun was confirmed during an excavation conducted due to redevelopment for residential housing. A row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} and an iron artifact burial structure were discovered, revealing a wealth of iron burial goods.
== Overview == The kofun was located west of the {{ill|Makuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft}}'s front section and just south of its associated {{ill|Jōgenjiyama Kofun|ja|浄元寺山古墳|12=draft}}, a satellite mound. In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣|12=draft}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=draft}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).
== References == * Nishihakayama Kofun: Furuichi Kofun Group Research Report III, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1997.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,0,98,146.html About Nishihakayama Kofun] (Fujidera City)

== Overview == The kofun was located west of the {{ill|Makuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft}}'s front section and just south of its associated {{ill|Jōgenjiyama Kofun|ja|浄元寺山古墳|12=draft}}, a satellite mound. In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣|12=draft}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=draft}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).
== References == * Nishihakayama Kofun: Furuichi Kofun Group Research Report III, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1997.
== External Links == * [http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,0,98,146.html About Nishihakayama Kofun] (Fujidera City)

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nishihakayama Kofun |image= |location=Osaka Prefecture, Fujidera City, Aoyama 1-chome |位置= |type=Square-shaped kofun |area=A11roximately 20m per side |built Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Iron artifact burial facility |Interred Persons= |burial goods=Numerous iron burial goods |Historic Site Designation= |Special Notes=Buried kofun }} '''Nishihakayama Kofun''' (西墓山古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Aoyama, [[Fujidera City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. The mound is presumed to have been square-shaped and is one of the kofun that typeed part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, a [[World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Historic Site site]].
The existence of the kofun was confirmed during an excavation conducted due to redevelopment for residential housing. A row of {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} and an iron artifact burial structure were discovered, revealing a wealth of iron burial goods.
== Overview == The kofun was located west of the {{ill|Makuyama Kofun|ja|墓山古墳|1=Hakayama Kofun|12=draft}}'s front section and just south of its associated {{ill|Jōgenjiyama Kofun|ja|浄元寺山古墳|12=draft}}, a satellite mound. In 1988, plans for replacing a wooden apartment complex with a reinforced concrete building prompted an excavation. Although no remains of the mound or related structures had been identified in this area, its proximity to Jōgenjiyama Kofun suggested the possibility of associated features such as moats or embankments, warranting a preliminary investigation. Shortly after the excavation began, a row of haniwa and an iron artifact burial facility were discovered.
=== Internal burial === [[File:Nishihakayama Kofun Shutaibu.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main burial area}}{{small|Exhibited at the [[Aisel Shura Hall|Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center]].}}]] The iron artifact burial facility was arranged in two rows: east and west. The eastern row measured 5.2 meters in length and 70–80 cm in width, containing over 232 iron burial goods, including [[tachi]] swords, {{ill|tsurugi|ja|剣|12=draft}} swords, short swords, spears, and halberds. The western row measured 6.2 meters in length and 60–80 cm in width, containing over 1,168 iron tools and weapons such as short swords, stabbing implements, plow tips, axes, knives, chisels, awls, saws, and more. Additionally, over 10 stone burial goods in the type of axes and at least one sickle-shaped stone artifact were found.
Analysis of the iron tools revealed that they were primarily ceremonial items. Plow tips, for example, often lacked functional edges, and knives were found to be imitations rather than practical tools. These findings suggest that the burial goods were created specifically for burial rituals and were not intended for practical use.
=== Haniwa and Dating === A row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} was unearthed, with 16 pieces discovered on the north side and 31 on the south. Numerous fragments of cylindrical, morning-glory-shaped, and figurative haniwa were also excavated. The cylindrical haniwa were encircled by triple bands, with a diameter of approximately 30 cm. Only one piece was complete, standing at 53.4 cm in height. The kofun is dated to the Kawanishi III sub-phase (early to mid-5th century). Figurative haniwa fragments included lid-shaped and house-shaped varieties.
The mound itself had been mostly leveled due to residential development. Aerial photographs from 1941 show the area already being used for farming, indicating that the mound was destroyed before that time.
=== Function of the Kofun === The absence of a burial chamber suggests that Nishihakayama Kofun may have served as a ceremonial satellite mound (fukusōyō baizuka) rather than a primary burial site. Similar satellite mounds are known in the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}, such as {{ill|Katomboyama Kofun|ja|カトンボ山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tsumakairi Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|1=Tsukamawari Kofun|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nanakamiyama Kofun|ja|七観山古墳|12=draft}}, as well as in the Furuichi Kofun Group, such as {{ill|Ariyama Kofun|ja|アリ山古墳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nochū Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}}. Although Nishihakayama Kofun no longer exists, a section of the iron artifact burial facility was preserved and is now exhibited at the {{ill|Aisel Shura Hall|ja|アイセルシュラホール|1=AICEL Shura Hall|12=draft}} (officially, Fujidera City Lifelong Learning Center).
== References == * Nishihakayama Kofun: Furuichi Kofun Group Research Report III, Fujidera City Board of Education, 1997.

18 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:西墓山古墳 up to rev 73368783
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 西墓山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市青山1丁目 |位置= |形状= 方墳 |規模=一辺20m前後 |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=鉄器埋納施設 |被葬者= |出土品=鉄器類多数 |史跡指定= |特記事項=埋没古墳 }} '''西墓山古墳'''（にしはかやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]青山にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]と推定される。[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]の[[古市古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。
住宅建て替え工事により発掘調査が行なわれて存在が明らかとなり、[[埴輪]]列が検出され、鉄器埋納遺構からは多数の鉄器類が出土している。
== 概要== [[墓山古墳]]の前方部西側にあり、同古墳の[[陪塚]]とされている浄元寺山古墳のすぐ南側において1988年に木造共同住宅を解体し、鉄筋へと建て替える工事が行なわれることが計画された。墳丘はおろか遺構、遺物の分布も確認されていなかった場所ではあったが、浄元寺山古墳のすぐ南に隣接するため、その関連施設(周濠や周濠の外堤など)の存在も予想されたため、事前の発掘調査が行なわれることとなった。調査が開始されるとすぐに、埴輪列と鉄器を埋納した遺構が検出された。 ===内部構造=== [[ファイル:西墓山古墳 主体部.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|主体部}}{{small|[[アイセルシュラホール|藤井寺市立生涯学習センター（アイセルシュラホール）]]展示。}}]] 鉄器埋納遺構は東西2列になっており、東列は長さ5.2m、幅70cm～80cmで、その範囲内に[[大刀]]、[[剣]]、短剣、ヤリ、ホコなど232点以上の鉄器が収められていた。西列は長さ6.2m、幅60cm～80cmで、短剣、刺突具、鋤先、斧、刀子、釶、異形釶、鑿、錐、鋸など1168点以上の鉄製品、斧形滑石製品10点以上、鎌形滑石製品1点以上が収められていた。埋納されていた鉄器の性格としては、鋤先の観察からは明瞭な刃部を持つものが極端に少なく、刀子も刃部を形成せず刀子を模倣する遺物が認められ実用には使えないものであった。またこれらの鉄器類には使用した痕跡も認められないため、埋納するために製作された儀礼的性格の鉄器類と考えられた。
===埴輪と年代=== 埴輪は[[円筒埴輪]]列となって北辺で16個体、南辺で31個体発掘され、また出土状況からみて当古墳に伴うと考えられる円筒、朝顔形 形象埴輪の破片が多数発掘されている。円筒埴輪は三条のタガを巡らし、[[法量#単なる「寸法」の意味での「法量」|法量]]は口径30cm前後、器高は判明する資料は1点のみで53.4cmであった。時期は川西編年のIII期新相（5世紀前半～中頃）に属するものであった。形象埴輪は蓋形埴輪と家形埴輪の細片が出土している。当古墳の墳丘は木造住宅の跡地であるため既に大半が削平されていた。また、1941年の墓山古墳付近の航空写真においては、その木造住宅が建つ以前の現地が写しだされているが、墳丘らしきものは見当たらず、すでに耕地となっていたことが判る。 ===古墳の性格=== 西墓山古墳の性格については当古墳の発掘調査範囲内において、人体埋葬施設の存在は確認できず、人体埋葬施設のない副葬用陪塚である可能性が考えられた。他に副葬用陪塚である可能性のある古墳は[[百舌鳥古墳群]]の[[カトンボ山古墳]]、[[塚廻古墳]] [[七観山古墳]]、[[古市古墳群]]ではアリ山古墳、[[野中古墳]]などが知られている。なお当古墳は調査後、消滅したが鉄製品埋納施設の一部が切り取られ、保存加工されて[[アイセルシュラホール]]（正式名 藤井寺市生涯学習センター）歴史展示室に展示されている。
==参考文献 == *『西墓山古墳 古市古墳群の調査研究報告III』 藤井寺市教育委員会 1997年
==外部リンク == *[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,0,98,146.html 西墓山古墳のこと]（藤井寺市）

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[西墓山古墳]] to [[Nishihayama Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 西墓山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府藤井寺市青山1丁目 |位置= |形状= 方墳 |規模=一辺20m前後 |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=鉄器埋納施設 |被葬者= |出土品=鉄器類多数 |史跡指定= |特記事項=埋没古墳 }} '''西墓山古墳'''（にしはかやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]青山にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]と推定される。[[文化遺産 (世界遺産)|世界文化遺産]]の[[古市古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。
住宅建て替え工事により発掘調査が行なわれて存在が明らかとなり、[[埴輪]]列が検出され、鉄器埋納遺構からは多数の鉄器類が出土している。
== 概要== [[墓山古墳]]の前方部西側にあり、同古墳の[[陪塚]]とされている浄元寺山古墳のすぐ南側において1988年に木造共同住宅を解体し、鉄筋へと建て替える工事が行なわれることが計画された。墳丘はおろか遺構、遺物の分布も確認されていなかった場所ではあったが、浄元寺山古墳のすぐ南に隣接するため、その関連施設(周濠や周濠の外堤など)の存在も予想されたため、事前の発掘調査が行なわれることとなった。調査が開始されるとすぐに、埴輪列と鉄器を埋納した遺構が検出された。 ===内部構造=== [[ファイル:西墓山古墳 主体部.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|主体部}}{{small|[[アイセルシュラホール|藤井寺市立生涯学習センター（アイセルシュラホール）]]展示。}}]] 鉄器埋納遺構は東西2列になっており、東列は長さ5.2m、幅70cm～80cmで、その範囲内に[[大刀]]、[[剣]]、短剣、ヤリ、ホコなど232点以上の鉄器が収められていた。西列は長さ6.2m、幅60cm～80cmで、短剣、刺突具、鋤先、斧、刀子、釶、異形釶、鑿、錐、鋸など1168点以上の鉄製品、斧形滑石製品10点以上、鎌形滑石製品1点以上が収められていた。埋納されていた鉄器の性格としては、鋤先の観察からは明瞭な刃部を持つものが極端に少なく、刀子も刃部を形成せず刀子を模倣する遺物が認められ実用には使えないものであった。またこれらの鉄器類には使用した痕跡も認められないため、埋納するために製作された儀礼的性格の鉄器類と考えられた。
===埴輪と年代=== 埴輪は[[円筒埴輪]]列となって北辺で16個体、南辺で31個体発掘され、また出土状況からみて当古墳に伴うと考えられる円筒、朝顔形 形象埴輪の破片が多数発掘されている。円筒埴輪は三条のタガを巡らし、[[法量#単なる「寸法」の意味での「法量」|法量]]は口径30cm前後、器高は判明する資料は1点のみで53.4cmであった。時期は川西編年のIII期新相（5世紀前半～中頃）に属するものであった。形象埴輪は蓋形埴輪と家形埴輪の細片が出土している。当古墳の墳丘は木造住宅の跡地であるため既に大半が削平されていた。また、1941年の墓山古墳付近の航空写真においては、その木造住宅が建つ以前の現地が写しだされているが、墳丘らしきものは見当たらず、すでに耕地となっていたことが判る。 ===古墳の性格=== 西墓山古墳の性格については当古墳の発掘調査範囲内において、人体埋葬施設の存在は確認できず、人体埋葬施設のない副葬用陪塚である可能性が考えられた。他に副葬用陪塚である可能性のある古墳は[[百舌鳥古墳群]]の[[カトンボ山古墳]]、[[塚廻古墳]] [[七観山古墳]]、[[古市古墳群]]ではアリ山古墳、[[野中古墳]]などが知られている。なお当古墳は調査後、消滅したが鉄製品埋納施設の一部が切り取られ、保存加工されて[[アイセルシュラホール]]（正式名 藤井寺市生涯学習センター）歴史展示室に展示されている。
==参考文献 == *『西墓山古墳 古市古墳群の調査研究報告III』 藤井寺市教育委員会 1997年
==外部リンク == *[http://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/9,0,98,146.html 西墓山古墳のこと]（藤井寺市）
